entries.context: Common mild insult used across Arabic-speaking countries when someone acts foolishly.
Casual insult targeting someone's intelligence. Used in everyday arguments across the Arab world.

entries.meaning: Dog — highly offensive in Arab culture
entries.context: Calling someone a dog is extremely offensive in Islamic culture. Never use this word. If heard directed at you, the person is extremely hostile.
entries.suggested_response: Leave immediately. Do not respond. Seek help in public spaces.

entries.context: Common insult implying someone is dumb and stubborn.
entries.suggested_response: Ignore. It's a light insult not worth escalating.

entries.meaning: Curse your religion — extreme religious insult
entries.context: One of the most severe insults in Muslim-majority regions. Involves cursing religion. Extremely hostile signal.
entries.suggested_response: Leave the area immediately. This is a serious threat indicator. Contact authorities.

entries.context: Accusation of theft. In some countries this can escalate quickly in public markets.
entries.suggested_response: Stay calm. Show your belongings. Say 'Ana mish harami' (I am not a thief).
